Fungal infection.description: A fungal infection occurs when a fungus invades a part of the body and the immune system cannot handle it.

medicines.name: Clotrimazole
medicines.dosage: Apply 1% cream twice daily for 2-4 weeks
medicines.notes: Clean and dry the affected area before application

medicines.name: Fluconazole
medicines.dosage: 150mg single dose or 50mg daily for 2 weeks
medicines.notes: Take with or without food. Avoid alcohol.

medicines.name: Terbinafine
medicines.notes: Monitor liver function for prolonged use

lifestyle: Keep affected areas clean and dry
Don't walk barefoot in public areas

Allergy.description: An allergy is an immune system response to a foreign substance that is not harmful to most people. Symptoms can range from mild to severe.

medicines.name: Cetirizine
medicines.notes: May cause drowsiness. Avoid alcohol.

medicines.name: Loratadine
medicines.notes: Preferred for daytime use

medicines.name: Fexofenadine
medicines.notes: Non-sedating antihistamine

medicines.name: Montelukast
medicines.notes: For allergy and asthma combined treatment

lifestyle: Keep windows closed during high pollen season

GERD.description: Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) is a chronic digestive disorder caused by stomach acid frequently flowing back into the esophagus.
precautions: Do not lie down immediately after eating

medicines.name: Omeprazole
medicines.notes: Take 30 minutes before meals for best effect

medicines.name: Pantoprazole
medicines.notes: May take 1-4 days to feel full effect

medicines.name: Ranitidine
medicines.notes: Take 30-60 minutes before meals

medicines.name: Antacids (Gaviscon)
medicines.notes: For immediate symptom relief

Chronic cholestasis.description: Chronic cholestasis is a condition where bile flow from the liver is reduced or blocked for prolonged periods, leading to bile buildup.

medicines.name: Ursodeoxycholic Acid
medicines.notes: Take with meals to minimize gastrointestinal effects

medicines.name: Cholestyramine
medicines.notes: Mix with water or juice. Take other medications 1 hour before.

medicines.name: Vitamin K supplement
medicines.notes: Monitor prothrombin time regularly

Drug Reaction.description: An adverse drug reaction is an unintended, harmful reaction to a medication taken at a normal dose.

medicines.name: Diphenhydramine
medicines.notes: For mild reactions. Causes significant drowsiness.

medicines.name: Prednisolone
medicines.notes: For moderate to severe reactions

medicines.name: Epinephrine (EpiPen)
medicines.notes: For anaphylaxis ONLY. Seek emergency care immediately after use.

lifestyle: Keep a list of all medications and allergies

Peptic ulcer disease.description: Peptic ulcer disease involves painful sores (ulcers) in the lining of the stomach, small intestine, or esophagus.

medicines.name: Omeprazole
medicines.notes: Take before meals. Continue for full prescribed course.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin + Clarithromycin
medicines.dosage: Amoxicillin 1g + Clarithromycin 500mg twice daily for 14 days
medicines.notes: Triple therapy with PPI for H. pylori eradication

medicines.name: Sucralfate
medicines.notes: Take on an empty stomach. Avoid antacids within 30 minutes.

AIDS.description: Acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) is a chronic, life-threatening condition caused by HIV that damages the immune system.

medicines.name: Tenofovir + Emtricitabine + Efavirenz
medicines.notes: Single-tablet regimen. Take at bedtime to reduce CNS side effects.

medicines.name: Bictegravir + FTC + TAF
medicines.dosage: One tablet once daily with food
medicines.notes: Modern first-line regimen with minimal side effects

medicines.name: Trimethoprim-Sulfamethoxazole
medicines.dosage: One DS tablet daily when CD4 < 200 cells/μL
medicines.notes: Prevents opportunistic infections like PCP

Diabetes.description: Diabetes is a chronic condition that affects how the body processes blood sugar (glucose), either through insufficient insulin production or insulin resistance.

medicines.name: Metformin
medicines.dosage: 500-2000mg daily in divided doses with meals
medicines.notes: Start low, increase gradually. Avoid in kidney disease.

medicines.name: Glipizide
medicines.notes: Monitor for hypoglycemia. Take 30 min before meals.

medicines.name: Insulin (Insulin Glargine)
medicines.notes: Dosage adjusted based on blood glucose monitoring

medicines.name: Sitagliptin
medicines.notes: Kidney-dose adjustment required

Gastroenteritis.description: Gastroenteritis is inflammation of the stomach and intestines, typically caused by bacteria or viral infection, resulting in diarrhea, vomiting and cramping.

medicines.name: Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS)
medicines.notes: Most important treatment. Prevent dehydration.

medicines.name: Loperamide
medicines.dosage: 4mg initially, then 2mg after each loose stool (max 16mg/day)
medicines.notes: Do not use if fever or bloody stool present

medicines.name: Ondansetron
medicines.notes: For controlling nausea and vomiting

Bronchial Asthma.description: Asthma is a condition that causes the airways to swell, narrow, and produce extra mucus, making it difficult to breathe.

medicines.name: Salbutamol (Albuterol)
medicines.dosage: 2 puffs (100mcg each) every 4-6 hours as needed
medicines.notes: Rescue inhaler for acute symptoms. Use spacer for best effect.

medicines.name: Budesonide
medicines.notes: Rinse mouth after use to prevent oral thrush

medicines.name: Montelukast
medicines.notes: Adjunct therapy. Not for acute attacks.

medicines.name: Salmeterol + Fluticasone
medicines.notes: For moderate-severe persistent asthma

Hypertension.description: Hypertension (high blood pressure) is a condition where blood pressure in the arteries is persistently elevated, a major risk factor for heart disease and stroke.

medicines.name: Amlodipine
medicines.notes: May cause ankle swelling. Take at same time each day.

medicines.name: Lisinopril
medicines.notes: Monitor potassium levels. Avoid in pregnancy.

medicines.name: Losartan
medicines.notes: Monitor kidney function and electrolytes

medicines.name: Hydrochlorothiazide
medicines.notes: Monitor electrolytes. Take in morning to avoid nocturia.

Migraine.description: A migraine is a severe headache often accompanied by nausea, vomiting, and extreme sensitivity to light and sound.

medicines.name: Sumatriptan
medicines.dosage: 50-100mg at onset, repeat after 2h if needed (max 200mg/day)
medicines.notes: Take at first sign of migraine. Not for prevention.

medicines.name: Ibuprofen
medicines.notes: Most effective when taken early. Take with food.

medicines.name: Propranolol
medicines.notes: For migraine prevention, not acute treatment

medicines.name: Topiramate
medicines.notes: Gradual dose increase to minimize side effects

Cervical spondylosis.description: Cervical spondylosis is age-related wear and tear of the spinal disks in the neck, causing neck pain, stiffness, and sometimes nerve compression.

medicines.name: Ibuprofen
medicines.notes: For pain and inflammation. Take with food.

medicines.name: Diclofenac
medicines.notes: Also available as topical gel for local application

medicines.name: Methocarbamol
medicines.notes: Can cause drowsiness. Avoid alcohol.

medicines.name: Gabapentin
medicines.notes: For nerve pain/radiculopathy component

Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).description: Paralysis from brain hemorrhage occurs when bleeding in the brain damages brain cells, causing loss of motor function.
precautions: Avoid blood thinners until stability confirmed

medicines.name: Nimodipine
medicines.notes: For vasospasm prevention in subarachnoid hemorrhage

medicines.name: Mannitol
medicines.notes: For acute intracranial pressure reduction

medicines.name: Levetiracetam
medicines.notes: For seizure prophylaxis

lifestyle: Psychological support for patient and family

Jaundice.description: Jaundice is a condition where the skin and whites of the eyes turn yellow due to elevated bilirubin levels in the blood.

medicines.name: Ursodeoxycholic Acid
medicines.notes: For cholestatic jaundice

medicines.name: Vitamin B12
medicines.notes: For nutritional support

medicines.name: Silymarin (Milk Thistle)
medicines.notes: Liver-protective supplement

Malaria.description: Malaria is a life-threatening disease caused by Plasmodium parasites transmitted through infected Anopheles mosquito bites.

medicines.name: Artemether + Lumefantrine (Coartem)
medicines.dosage: 4 tablets twice daily for 3 days (with food)
medicines.notes: First-line treatment. Take with fatty food for better absorption.

medicines.name: Chloroquine
medicines.notes: For chloroquine-sensitive P. vivax or P. malariae

medicines.name: Primaquine
medicines.notes: For radical cure of P. vivax/ovale. Test G6PD first!

lifestyle: Seek treatment immediately if symptoms develop

Chicken pox.description: Chickenpox is a highly contagious viral infection causing an itchy blistery rash, fever, and fatigue, caused by the varicella-zoster virus.

medicines.name: Acyclovir
medicines.notes: Most effective if started within 24-48h of rash onset

medicines.name: Calamine Lotion
medicines.notes: For itch relief. Do not use on face or near eyes.

medicines.name: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen)
medicines.notes: For fever. Avoid aspirin in children.

medicines.name: Cetirizine
medicines.notes: To reduce itching

lifestyle: Stay home until all blisters have crusted

Dengue.description: Dengue is a mosquito-borne viral disease causing high fever, severe joint and muscle pain, rash, and may progress to severe bleeding.
precautions: Seek immediate care if platelet count drops

medicines.name: Paracetamol
medicines.notes: FIRST-CHOICE. Avoid aspirin and ibuprofen which increase bleeding risk.

medicines.name: Oral Rehydration Salts
medicines.notes: Crucial for preventing dengue shock syndrome

medicines.name: Platelet transfusion
medicines.dosage: As prescribed by physician when platelets < 10,000
medicines.notes: Hospital-based treatment for severe dengue

lifestyle: Drink plenty of fluids (water, coconut water, juices)
Monitor for warning signs (bleeding, severe abdominal pain)

Typhoid.description: Typhoid fever is a bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi, spread through contaminated food and water.

medicines.name: Azithromycin
medicines.notes: Preferred outpatient treatment for uncomplicated typhoid

medicines.name: Ceftriaxone
medicines.notes: For severe/complicated cases requiring hospitalization

medicines.name: Ciprofloxacin
medicines.notes: Check local resistance patterns before prescribing

lifestyle: Drink only boiled or bottled water

Hepatitis A.description: Hepatitis A is a liver infection caused by the Hepatitis A virus (HAV), spread through contaminated food, water, or close contact.

medicines.name: Paracetamol (low dose)
medicines.dosage: 500mg every 6-8 hours (limit to 2g/day due to liver affection)
medicines.notes: For fever and discomfort. Reduced dose due to liver disease.

medicines.name: Ondansetron
medicines.notes: For nausea and vomiting

medicines.name: Vitamin B Complex
medicines.notes: To support recovery and liver function

Hepatitis B.description: Hepatitis B is a viral liver infection transmitted through blood, sexual contact, or from mother to baby. Chronic infection can lead to cirrhosis.

medicines.name: Tenofovir disoproxil fumarate
medicines.notes: First-line antiviral for chronic Hepatitis B

medicines.name: Entecavir
medicines.notes: Take on empty stomach. High barrier to resistance.

medicines.name: Pegylated Interferon alfa-2a
medicines.notes: For selected patients; multiple side effects

Hepatitis C.description: Hepatitis C is a viral liver infection primarily spread through blood. Chronic infection leads to liver damage, cirrhosis, and liver cancer.

medicines.name: Sofosbuvir + Velpatasvir
medicines.dosage: One tablet (400mg/100mg) once daily for 12 weeks
medicines.notes: Pan-genotypic regimen. Cure rate >95%.

medicines.name: Glecaprevir + Pibrentasvir
medicines.dosage: 3 tablets once daily for 8-12 weeks with food
medicines.notes: For all genotypes. Highly effective pan-genotypic regimen.

medicines.name: Ribavirin
medicines.notes: Combined with other antivirals in difficult-to-treat cases

lifestyle: Regular monitoring of viral load and liver enzymes

Hepatitis D.description: Hepatitis D is a liver infection that only affects people with Hepatitis B. It is the most severe form of hepatitis.

medicines.name: Pegylated Interferon alfa
medicines.notes: Currently only effective treatment for chronic hepatitis D

medicines.name: Bulevirtide
medicines.notes: Newly approved entry inhibitor for hepatitis D

lifestyle: Hepatitis B vaccination to prevent co-infection

Hepatitis E.description: Hepatitis E is a liver disease caused by the Hepatitis E virus (HEV), primarily transmitted through contaminated drinking water.

medicines.name: Ribavirin
medicines.notes: For chronic HEV in immunocompromised. Avoid in pregnancy.

medicines.name: Supportive care (IV fluids)
medicines.notes: Most acute cases resolve without specific antiviral therapy

medicines.name: Vitamin supplements
medicines.notes: To support liver recovery

lifestyle: Eat cooked food from safe sources

Alcoholic hepatitis.description: Alcoholic hepatitis is liver inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, leading to liver damage.

medicines.name: Prednisolone
medicines.dosage: 40mg once daily for 4 weeks, then taper
medicines.notes: For severe alcoholic hepatitis (Maddrey's Discriminant Function > 32)

medicines.name: N-Acetylcysteine (NAC)
medicines.dosage: 150mg/kg IV loading, then maintenance as prescribed
medicines.notes: Adjunct therapy; improves short-term survival

medicines.name: Thiamine (Vitamin B1)
medicines.dosage: 100mg IV or IM for 3 days, then oral
medicines.notes: Essential to prevent Wernicke's encephalopathy in alcoholics

Tuberculosis.description: Tuberculosis (TB) is a contagious bacterial infection caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, primarily affecting the lungs.

medicines.name: HRZE Regimen (Isoniazid + Rifampicin + Pyrazinamide + Ethambutol)
medicines.dosage: Weight-based dosing for 2 months intensive phase, then HR for 4 months
medicines.notes: NEVER take partial treatment. Full 6-month course mandatory.

medicines.name: Pyridoxine (Vitamin B6)
medicines.notes: Prevents isoniazid-induced peripheral neuropathy

Common Cold.description: The common cold is a viral infection of the upper respiratory tract, primarily caused by rhinoviruses.
precautions: Avoid close contact with infected persons

medicines.name: Paracetamol
medicines.notes: For fever and body aches

medicines.name: Cetirizine
medicines.notes: For runny nose and sneezing

medicines.name: Pseudoephedrine
medicines.notes: Do not use for more than 7 days. Avoid in hypertension.

medicines.name: Zinc lozenges
medicines.notes: Start within 24 hours of symptoms. May reduce duration.

lifestyle: Honey and lemon in warm water for sore throat

Pneumonia.description: Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin
medicines.notes: First-line for mild community-acquired pneumonia

medicines.name: Azithromycin
medicines.dosage: 500mg on day 1, then 250mg daily for 4 days
medicines.notes: For atypical pneumonia or penicillin allergy

medicines.name: Ceftriaxone + Azithromycin
medicines.dosage: Ceftriaxone 1-2g IV daily + Azithromycin 500mg IV daily
medicines.notes: Standard inpatient regimen for moderate-severe CAP

lifestyle: Breathing exercises once acute phase passes

Dimorphic hemmorhoids (piles).description: Hemorrhoids (piles) are swollen veins in the lower rectum and anus, causing discomfort, itching, and sometimes bleeding.

medicines.name: Hydrocortisone + Lidocaine cream
medicines.dosage: Apply to affected area 2-4 times daily
medicines.notes: For temporary relief. Do not use for more than 7 days continuously.

medicines.name: Psyllium Husk (Isabgol)
medicines.notes: Bulk-forming laxative to soften stool

medicines.name: Diosmin + Hesperidin (Daflon)
medicines.notes: Reduces bleeding and discomfort in hemorrhoids

Heart attack.description: A heart attack (myocardial infarction) occurs when blood flow to part of the heart is blocked, causing heart muscle damage.
precautions: Do NOT drive yourself to hospital

medicines.name: Aspirin
medicines.notes: EMERGENCY: chew (not swallow whole) immediately when heart attack is suspected

medicines.name: Dual Antiplatelet: Aspirin + Clopidogrel/Ticagrelor
medicines.dosage: Aspirin 100mg + Ticagrelor 90mg twice daily for 12 months
medicines.notes: Post-MI treatment to prevent recurrent events

medicines.name: Atorvastatin
medicines.notes: Start immediately post-MI. Take at bedtime.

medicines.name: Metoprolol
medicines.notes: Cardioprotective. Monitor heart rate (keep >60 bpm).

Varicose veins.description: Varicose veins are enlarged, twisted veins near the skin surface caused by weak or damaged vein valves.

medicines.name: Diosmin + Hesperidin
medicines.notes: Reduces symptoms of chronic venous insufficiency

medicines.name: Horse Chestnut Extract (Escin)
medicines.notes: May reduce leg swelling and heaviness

lifestyle: Elevate legs 30 minutes several times a day

Hypothyroidism.description: Hypothyroidism occurs when the thyroid gland produces insufficient thyroid hormone, slowing metabolism and causing fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance.
precautions: Take medication at consistent time daily

medicines.name: Levothyroxine (T4)
medicines.notes: Take on empty stomach 30-60 min before breakfast. Separate from calcium/iron.

medicines.name: Liothyronine (T3)
medicines.dosage: 25mcg daily (in combination with T4 in select cases)
medicines.notes: For patients who feel better on T3+T4 combination therapy

lifestyle: Avoid excessive soy and cruciferous vegetables

Hyperthyroidism.description: Hyperthyroidism is when the thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, accelerating metabolism with symptoms like weight loss, rapid heartbeat, and anxiety.

medicines.name: Methimazole (Carbimazole)
medicines.dosage: 10-30mg daily, adjusted to lowest effective dose
medicines.notes: First-line treatment. Check CBC for agranulocytosis if fever/sore throat.

medicines.name: Propylthiouracil (PTU)
medicines.notes: Preferred in first trimester pregnancy. More frequent dosing than methimazole.

medicines.name: Propranolol
medicines.notes: For symptomatic relief: heart rate, tremor, anxiety. Not a definitive treatment.

lifestyle: Avoid foods high in iodine (seaweed, large amounts of seafood)

Hypoglycemia.description: Hypoglycemia is abnormally low blood sugar (glucose), which can cause symptoms ranging from shakiness to seizures and loss of consciousness.

medicines.name: Glucose tablets/gel
medicines.dosage: 15-20g orally for mild hypoglycemia, repeat in 15 min if needed
medicines.'15-15 Rule': 15g of fast carbs, check sugar after 15 min.

medicines.name: Glucagon Kit
medicines.dosage: 1mg IM or SC for severe hypoglycemia (unconscious patient)
medicines.notes: Emergency use. Give when patient cannot swallow. Call ambulance.

medicines.name: Dextrose (D50W)
medicines.notes: Hospital-based treatment for severe hypoglycemia

lifestyle: Educate family members on glucagon use

Osteoarthritis.description: Osteoarthritis is the most common form of arthritis, caused by worn-down cartilage in joints, primarily affecting the knees, hips, and hands.

medicines.name: Paracetamol
medicines.notes: First-choice for mild-moderate OA pain

medicines.name: Ibuprofen / Diclofenac
medicines.notes: Take with food. Monitor renal function and BP.

medicines.name: Glucosamine + Chondroitin
medicines.notes: May provide modest benefit for knee OA. Allow 2-3 months for effect.

medicines.name: Intra-articular Corticosteroid injection
medicines.notes: For acute flare-ups. Max 3-4 injections per year.

Arthritis.description: Arthritis is inflammation of one or more joints, causing pain, swelling, and stiffness. Rheumatoid arthritis is an autoimmune form.

medicines.name: Methotrexate
medicines.notes: Take with folic acid. Regular liver and blood monitoring essential.

medicines.name: Hydroxychloroquine
medicines.notes: Annual eye exams required. Takes 3-6 months for full effect.

medicines.name: Etanercept / Adalimumab
medicines.notes: For moderate-severe RA not responding to conventional DMARDs

medicines.name: Prednisolone
medicines.dosage: 5-15mg daily initially, taper to lowest effective dose
medicines.notes: Short-term use for flare control

Urinary tract infection.description: A urinary tract infection (UTI) is an infection in any part of the urinary system, most commonly the bladder and urethra.

medicines.name: Nitrofurantoin
medicines.dosage: 100mg modified-release twice daily for 5-7 days with food
medicines.notes: Do not use if eGFR < 45. Take with food.

medicines.name: Trimethoprim
medicines.notes: Check local resistance patterns

medicines.name: Fosfomycin
medicines.dosage: 3g sachet single dose dissolved in water
medicines.notes: Convenient single-dose treatment for uncomplicated UTI

medicines.name: Ciprofloxacin
medicines.notes: Reserve for complicated UTI or treatment failures

lifestyle: Urinate when you feel the urge

Psoriasis.description: Psoriasis is a chronic autoimmune condition causing rapid skin cell buildup, resulting in scaling, red patches, and itching.

medicines.name: Betamethasone dipropionate
medicines.dosage: Apply thin layer once or twice daily to affected areas
medicines.notes: High-potency steroid. Avoid on face and skin folds. Do not use long-term.

medicines.name: Calcipotriol
medicines.notes: Safe for long-term use. Use separately from corticosteroids.

medicines.name: Methotrexate
medicines.notes: For moderate-severe psoriasis. Regular blood and liver monitoring.

medicines.name: Adalimumab / Secukinumab
medicines.notes: For severe psoriasis not responding to other treatments

Impetigo.description: Impetigo is a highly contagious skin infection that mainly affects children, causing red sores that rupture and form honey-colored crusts.

medicines.name: Mupirocin (Bactroban) cream
medicines.dosage: Apply to affected areas 3 times daily for 5-7 days
medicines.notes: First-line for localized impetigo. Clean area before application.

medicines.name: Fusidic acid cream
medicines.notes: Alternative topical treatment

medicines.name: Flucloxacillin
medicines.notes: For widespread impetigo or failure of topical treatment

lifestyle: Avoid touching or picking at sores
Stay home from school until sores crust over
